title: SwiftUI Layout Protocol: Building a Custom Grid From Scratch
abstract: We cover the SwiftUI Layout protocol introduced in iOS 16: implementing sizeThatFits and placeSubviews, working with LayoutSubviews and ProposedViewSize. Using a custom masonry grid as an example, we build a placement algorithm with variable cell heights. We compare performance with LazyVGrid through the Instruments SwiftUI template, discuss the subtleties of Layout cache and the specifics of ViewBuilder under the hood.

title: Reducing iOS Application Binary Size from 220 to 90 MB
abstract: A story of fighting bloat in a banking application binary. We analyze the Mach-O dump through linkmap and Bloaty, remove unused symbols through -dead_strip and LTO. We optimize dyld closure, move heavy resources to On-Demand Resources, cut legacy localizations. The finale is a comparison of App Store download size before and after optimization, minus 60% for the user.

title: ARKit with Metal Shaders: Virtual Makeup Try-On
abstract: How we built virtual try-on for lipstick and eyeshadow through ARKit Face Tracking and custom Metal shaders. We discuss ARFaceAnchor with blend shapes, texture mapping onto the face mesh, realistic blending with physical lighting. We solve the problem of tracking stabilization on devices without TrueDepth, building a fallback to classical VIO with landmark detection.

title: Parallel XCUITest on 40 Simulators in CI
abstract: Experience of building parallel XCUITest automation for a large application. We configure xcodebuild test with -parallel-testing-enabled and Test Plans, shard tests through Test Identifiers. We solve the problem of flaky tests through retry logic and snapshot testing on swift-snapshot-testing. We reduced regression run duration from 2.5 hours to 14 minutes.

title: iOS Application Accessibility: Beyond VoiceOver
abstract: Accessibility is not just about VoiceOver. We cover Switch Control for people with motor impairments, Voice Control with custom commands, Differentiate Without Color for color blindness. We show UIAccessibilityCustomAction for complex controls, support of Dynamic Type up to AccessibilityXXXLarge. Using an insurance application as an example, we conduct an audit through Accessibility Inspector and fix 30 critical findings.

title: Secure Enclave and Biometrics: Protecting Keys in a Banking Application
abstract: How to properly work with Secure Enclave for storing cryptographic keys in a banking application. We cover kSecAttrTokenIDSecureEnclave, generation of EC keys inside SE, transaction signing with biometric authentication through LAContext. We discuss jailbreak protection through runtime checks, SSL pinning through CertificatePinner, detection of Frida injects in production.

title: Bazel for iOS: Modularization with Remote Cache
abstract: Experience of migrating an Xcode workspace of 150 modules to Bazel. We cover rules_swift and rules_apple, writing custom Bazel rules for resource generation. We set up a remote cache in S3 with buildbuddy, accelerate clean build from 22 to 5 minutes. A separate block is about signing and distribution through Fastlane match plus the App Store Connect API.

title: WebSocket and URLProtocol: The Network Stack of a Video Player
abstract: Architecture of the network layer of a streaming application. We use URLSessionWebSocketTask for the real-time event channel with reconnect through exponential backoff and jitter. We implement a custom URLProtocol for intercepting HTTP requests of the player, add edge logic for caching HLS manifests. We integrate Apollo iOS for the move from REST to GraphQL for the content catalog.

title: The Composable Architecture in Production: The Payments Module
abstract: Experience of introducing The Composable Architecture in a new payments module. We cover Reducer, Store, Effect and dependency injection through @DependencyClient. We test feature logic through TestStore with deterministic effect handling. We discuss reducer composition, navigation through @Reducer destinations. We honestly speak about the entry barrier for the team and compile-time costs.

title: Swift 6 Strict Concurrency: Migrating a Feature Module Without Pain
abstract: How we migrated 30 thousand lines of code to Swift 6 strict concurrency. We cover the Sendable protocol, isolation through actor, MainActor for UI code. We solve typical errors: capture self in Task, passing non-Sendable between actors, migration of delegate protocols. We discuss the strategy of step-by-step enabling strict concurrency through language mode and SWIFT_STRICT_CONCURRENCY=complete.

title: Compose Modifier and Custom Layout: Animations That Don't Lag
abstract: A deep dive into the Modifier system in Jetpack Compose. We write a custom Layout through MeasureScope, cover Modifier.layout vs Modifier.composed. We use Modifier.animateContentSize and SharedTransitionLayout for smooth transitions. Using a marketplace product card as an example, we show how to avoid unnecessary recompositions through Stable, Immutable annotations and derivedStateOf.

title: ART, JIT and AOT: What Happens After APK Installation
abstract: What actually happens when a user installs an APK. We cover the Android Runtime: dex2oat compilation, profile-guided AOT, JIT with tiered compilation. We explain how Google Play uses Cloud Profiles to optimize starts. We show how to read /system/bin/oatdump and analyze speed-profile. Using a banking application as an example, we find a performance regression after updating to Android 14.

title: Room with FTS5: Full-Text Search in a Messenger
abstract: How we introduced full-text search in a messenger with 8 million active users through Room with FTS5. We cover the @Fts5 entity, tokenizers for Russian with dictionary normalization, ranking through bm25. We maintain synchronization of the FTS virtual table with the main one through triggers. We solve the problem of migration from FTS4 without re-indexing the entire database.

title: Wear OS 5: A Fitness Application with Minimal Power Consumption
abstract: We build an application for smartwatches on Wear OS 5 with Compose for Wear. We use the Health Services API for continuous heart rate and SpO2 readings with minimal battery drain. We implement ambient mode with low-bit graphics, support Tiles with Protolayout. We show how to test on the emulator and real Pixel Watch and Galaxy Watch models.

title: Android Auto: Navigation Through Car App Library
abstract: We build a navigation application for Android Auto. We cover the Car App Library, Templates API with MapTemplate and NavigationTemplate, the constraints of the Distraction-Optimized interface. We implement routing through the Mapbox Navigation SDK with offline maps. We integrate Voice Actions through Assistant App Actions, test on the DHU and real head units.

title: Macrobenchmark and Baseline Profiles: Removing Jank in Streaming
abstract: How we reduced janky frames percentage from 4.2 to 0.6 in a video-on-demand application. We use Macrobenchmark for regression testing of start and scroll, collect Baseline and Startup Profiles. We enable R8 fullMode, optimize Compose rendering through Modifier.drawBehind. We analyze traces in Perfetto, look for shader compilation jank through Android GPU Inspector.

title: Play Integrity and Hardware-Backed KeyStore: Protecting a Banking Application
abstract: We protect the Android banking application from device compromise. We use Play Integrity API for verdicts about the state of the device, application and account. We store keys in Hardware-backed KeyStore with the StrongBox flag, protect with biometrics through BiometricPrompt with CryptoObject. We detect Frida and Magisk through a combination of system checks, obfuscate code through R8 with ProGuard rules.

title: Gradle Configuration Cache and Project Isolation
abstract: We accelerate Android project builds in Gradle 8.10. We enable Configuration Cache, cover typical serializability and tasks-configuration errors. We connect Project Isolation for parallel module configuration. We set up Gradle Enterprise with remote build cache in S3 and build scans. On a real project with 50 developers we reduce incremental build from 80 to 18 seconds.

title: gRPC and OkHttp: A Network Stack for Bidirectional Order Streaming
abstract: Architecture of the network layer of a food delivery application. We move to gRPC through grpc-kotlin for bidirectional streaming of order status updates. We configure OkHttp with custom Interceptor for authentication, retry logic through ExponentialBackoff. We optimize TLS handshake through session resumption, profile DNS resolution and connection pool through OkHttp EventListener.

title: Kotlin Multiplatform: A Shared Module for iOS and Android
abstract: Experience of extracting business logic of a social network into a Kotlin Multiplatform shared module. We cover expect/actual for platform-dependent code, using Ktor for networking and SQLDelight for persistence. We integrate the shared module into iOS through CocoaPods Multiplatform plugin and SwiftKlib. We honestly speak about trade-offs: binary size on iOS, debugging complexity through iOS hosts, lack of native interop.

title: GraalVM Native Image: Migrating Spring Boot 3 to Native
abstract: Experience of migrating critical bank microservices to GraalVM Native Image. We cover reachability metadata, build-time vs runtime initialization, the specifics of reflection and proxy in native. We reduce cold start from 8 seconds to 60 milliseconds, memory consumption from 480 to 120 MB. We discuss pitfalls: dynamic proxies in Spring Data, JSONB mapping in Hibernate, JNI restrictions.

title: Go and pprof: Hunting Allocations in a High-Load API
abstract: Profiling a Go service under load of 30 thousand RPS. We cover working with net/http/pprof, running heap, allocs, mutex and block profiles. We analyze flame graphs in pprof, look for hot paths and suboptimal allocations. We apply sync.Pool for buffer reuse, switch to stream processing through bufio.Scanner. We reduce p99 latency from 280 to 65 milliseconds.

title: io_uring in Rust: A Trading Gateway With Latency Under 100 µs
abstract: Architecture of an exchange gateway in Rust with io_uring through tokio-uring and glommio. We cover submission queue, completion queue, ring buffers. We compare performance with epoll on the FIX protocol, measure latency through DPDK and DAQ instruments. We apply lock-free queues through crossbeam, zero-copy parsing through bytes::Bytes, fight against GC pauses through mimalloc.

title: Python 3.13 Without GIL: What Has Actually Changed
abstract: We cover PEP 703, the experimental no-Global-Interpreter-Lock mode of Python 3.13. We compare performance of multi-threaded CPU-bound tasks with no-GIL and PEP 684 subinterpreters. We test on a real API on FastAPI and a Celery orchestrator. We discuss compatibility with C extensions, the state of NumPy and PyTorch in no-GIL mode, race condition risks in existing code.

title: PostgreSQL Patroni and pgBouncer: A Cluster of 24 Nodes
abstract: Maintenance of a production PostgreSQL cluster at a logistics holding. We cover the configuration of Patroni with DCS on etcd, automatic failover, synchronous and asynchronous replication. We configure pgBouncer in transaction mode for 8 thousand clients per node, balance reads through HAProxy. We discuss the subtleties of logical replication between data centers through pglogical, monitoring of replication lag.

title: Kafka Streams and Schema Registry: Exactly-Once in Billing
abstract: Architecture of a billing pipeline on Kafka Streams. We implement exactly-once through transactional producer and read_committed consumer. We support Avro schema evolution through Confluent Schema Registry with FORWARD_TRANSITIVE compatibility. We cover windowed aggregations with RocksDB state stores, recovery through standby replicas. Using processing of 200 thousand events per second as an example, we show monitoring through Burrow and kafka-lag-exporter.

title: eBPF for Observability: Continuous Profiling Without Agents
abstract: Continuous profiling of production services through eBPF without changing application code. We cover how Pixie and Parca work: kprobes, uprobes, perf events. We collect CPU and off-CPU profiles, off-CPU stack traces for I/O-bound tasks. We integrate with Pyroscope for aggregation, compare with traditional pprof. We discuss the limitations: overhead on high-frequency probing, requirements for Linux kernel 5.10+.

title: OAuth 2.1 and DPoP: Protecting Public APIs
abstract: Modern protection of public APIs through OAuth 2.1 and DPoP. We cover the move from implicit flow to PKCE for SPAs, sender-constrained tokens through DPoP-Proof. We set up Keycloak with custom Token Mapper, implement token introspection through RFC 7662. We compare JWT and opaque tokens, discuss storage of refresh tokens in HttpOnly cookies. We integrate mTLS between internal services through Istio.

title: Envoy and xDS: Dynamic Routing Without Restarts
abstract: Dynamic configuration of Envoy through xDS API: CDS, EDS, RDS, LDS. We write a custom control-plane on Go with ADS support. We implement canary routing through weighted clusters with automatic traffic escalation. We connect custom Lua filters for authorization and rate-limiting. Using 14 public APIs as an example, we show operation without gateway restarts and hot config reload.

title: Raft With Pre-Vote and Witness Replicas: Production Lessons
abstract: Implementation of Raft with extensions for a distributed OLTP database. We cover pre-vote for fighting network partitions, witness replicas for cross-region clusters. We discuss linearizable reads through ReadIndex, lease-based optimization for the leader. We analyze clock skew between regions, the impact on linearizability safety. We share Jepsen testing lessons: which bugs we found, how we fixed them, what changed in the design.

title: RAG in Production: Hybrid Search and Long Context
abstract: Experience of building a RAG system on top of internal documentation. We cover hybrid BM25 + embeddings search in Qdrant with reciprocal rank fusion. We use a 7B model with YaRN for 128k context, fine-tuned LoRA on our own Q&A. We deployed vLLM with PagedAttention and continuous batching, measure p95 latency and cost per token. We compare with the long-context approach without retrieval.

title: SAM and YOLOv8: Automatic Categorization of Millions of Products
abstract: How we automated categorization of an e-commerce catalog of 12 million SKUs. We use YOLOv8 for object detection on photos, Segment Anything for segmentation, ControlNet for cover generation. We fine-tuned a classifier on 4 million annotated images with a long-tail class distribution. We discuss active learning, weak supervision and quality control of annotation through duplicated tasks.

title: Feature Store on Feast: Iceberg + DragonflyDB
abstract: A platform feature store on Feast for 60 models at an insurance holding. Offline storage in Apache Iceberg on S3, online storage in DragonflyDB as a Redis replacement with better throughput. We cover point-in-time joins without data leakage, materialization through Spark. We support feature monitoring through Evidently, A/B testing with automatic stop on metric degradation.

title: Two-Tower Recommendations in Vespa: 40 Million DAU
abstract: Architecture of the recommender system of a streaming service. A two-stage pipeline: candidate generation on a two-tower model through ANN search in Vespa, ranking on gradient boosting and transformers. We discuss in-batch negatives, hard negative mining, calibration through temperature scaling. We solve cold-start through content-based fallback, fight against popularity bias through debiasing techniques from Pinterest and Spotify works.

title: PPO and SAC for a Manipulator: Sim-to-Real in Robotics
abstract: Training a manipulator policy through PPO and SAC in NVIDIA Isaac simulation. We cover reward shaping for pick-and-place tasks, domain randomization for sim-to-real transfer. We compare on-policy and off-policy approaches, move to offline RL through CQL and IQL for fine-tuning on demonstrations. On a real UR5e we show the policy transfer, discuss the gap between simulation and reality.

title: CatBoost in Credit Scoring: Features, Drift, Monitoring
abstract: Experience of building credit risk models at a credit scoring company. We use CatBoost with categorical features and grouping, fight against overfitting through early stopping and ordered boosting. We support PSI drift monitoring across 200 features, automatic retraining on threshold violation. We compare with TabNet and FT-Transformer on the same data, discuss interpretability through SHAP.

title: Edge ML: Quantization on Jetson Orin and Hailo-8
abstract: Deployment of detection models on edge devices for smart security cameras. We compare NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano with Hailo-8 on YOLO batch inference. We do int8 quantization through TensorRT with post-training calibration, prune layers through torch.nn.utils.prune to a target FLOPs budget. We measure mAP before and after optimization, p99 latency on a batch of 8. The finale is practical recommendations on hardware choice for different scenarios.

title: Apache Iceberg and Spark Photon: Lakehouse at a Bank
abstract: Architecture of a data lakehouse at a bank on Apache Iceberg in S3. We cover the structure of Iceberg: metadata files, snapshots, time travel. We use Spark with the Photon engine for batch pipelines, support 200+ models in dbt. We migrate from Hive Metastore to Unity Catalog with row-level security and tag-based access control. We discuss compaction, expire snapshots, file size optimization.

title: CUPED and Causal Analysis in Product Analytics
abstract: A/B test design at a game studio. We apply CUPED for variance reduction, accelerate achieving statistical power by 40%. We cover bootstrap for interval estimates of non-additive metrics. We apply difference-in-differences for evaluating marketing campaigns without a control group, instrumental variables for anti-cannibalization analysis. On real cases we show typical p-hacking and multiple testing errors.

title: Ray and MIG Partitioning: How to Share A100 Between Experiments
abstract: An ML research platform on 80 A100 nodes. We set up a Ray Cluster with autoscaling on top of Slurm, move part of the workloads from pure Slurm to Ray Tune for hyperparameter optimization. We use MIG partitioning for sharing cards between small experiments. We optimize NCCL for efficient all-reduce on InfiniBand. We compare with Kubeflow and discuss when to choose which tool.

title: React 19 Server Actions and Feature-Sliced Design
abstract: Architecture of a React application of a marketplace with 15 million DAU. We use Server Actions for mutations without a separate API layer, TanStack Query for server state, Zustand for client state. We have introduced feature-sliced design with a clear boundary between shared, entities, features, widgets. We support strict typing through TypeScript 5 with branded types to prevent mixing UserId and ProductId.

title: Nuxt 3 and Vue 3.5: Updating an Analytics SaaS
abstract: Experience of updating an analytics SaaS on Nuxt 3 with Vue 3.5. We use Reactive Props Destructure to simplify components, useTemplateRef instead of ref functions. We cover caching through Nitro and server routes, hybrid rendering with ISR for user dashboards. We apply Pinia for global state, Vue Suspense for asynchronous components with granular loading states.

title: Next.js Partial Prerendering for EdTech
abstract: How we introduced Partial Prerendering in an EdTech application on Next.js 15. The static shell of the course page is generated at build time, dynamic parts (reviews, progress, recommendations) are streamed through Suspense. We cover the subtleties of unstable_cache, tag-based revalidation, the revalidatePath helpers. We discuss how to properly work with Server Actions for form mutations and progressive enhancement.

title: PWA with Workbox: Offline Mode for a Restaurant Chain
abstract: We build a PWA for a restaurant chain with a full offline mode. We use Workbox for precaching and runtime caching, support Web Push for order status notifications. We implement Background Sync for deferred order submission on connection loss. We integrate Web Share API, Payment Request API. We test on real devices through WebPageTest and BrowserStack, measure impact on Core Web Vitals.

title: WebGL and Shader Scenes: An Interactive Site for a Brand
abstract: We build an interactive site with shader graphics through three.js. We cover custom GLSL shaders for a molten metal effect, post-processing through EffectComposer. We use GSAP for synchronizing scroll with timelines, Framer Motion for UI animations. We optimize performance: instanced rendering, LOD, jank measurement through PerformanceObserver and the Long Tasks API.

title: INP and scheduler.yield: Unloading the Main Thread
abstract: How we reduced Interaction to Next Paint on the home page of a news portal from 380 to 140 milliseconds. We cover task scheduling through scheduler.yield and scheduler.postTask with priorities. We move heavy computations to Web Workers through Comlink, optimize hydration through React 18 useTransition. We introduced RUM on CrUX and SpeedCurve, monitor INP at the 75th percentile in production.

title: Accessibility Through axe-core and Playwright in CI
abstract: We automate accessibility testing at a financial holding. We integrate axe-core with Playwright for page checks, set up pa11y for static analysis. We test with NVDA, JAWS and VoiceOver on real scenarios, support compliance with WCAG 2.2 AA. We cover complex patterns: combobox with aria-controls, roving tabindex for menus, focus trap for modals. We share examples of 30 critical fixes.

title: Vite 5 and Nx: Accelerating a Monorepo of 40 Packages
abstract: Migration of a SaaS company monorepo of 40 packages from Webpack to Vite 5. We reduced dev build time from 28 to 3 seconds, hot reload from 4 seconds to 200 milliseconds. We support Nx with distributed task execution, cache results in Nx Cloud. We experiment with Turbopack for critical scenarios. We discuss migration pitfalls: ESM-only dependencies, differences in CSS modules processing.

title: Design Tokens by W3C and Style Dictionary: Figma Into Code
abstract: Design system of a bank for 80+ product teams. We support 220 React components with strict typing, document through Storybook 8 with visual regression on Chromatic. We work with design tokens by the W3C spec, sync Figma and code through Style Dictionary with custom transformers. We support dark theme through CSS custom properties and color-mix.

title: Native Federation on Vite: Evolution of Micro-Frontends
abstract: Evolution of micro-frontends at an insurance company from Webpack Module Federation to Native Federation on Vite. We cover import maps, Module Federation 2.0 evolution, runtime composition through ESM. We discuss the move from runtime composition to edge side includes through Cloudflare Workers for latency-critical scenarios. We describe team contracts through Pact, test independent deployments in CI.
